description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dark green and gray fuselage, featuring two stacked wings connected by struts and wires. The aircraft has a wooden two-blade propeller mounted at the nose, and the engine is partially exposed behind the propeller. Distinctive black-and-white cross insignias are visible on the wings and fuselage, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The tail section is rounded with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and visible seat.
description: A vintage green and gray biplane with a wooden propeller, open cockpit, and military cross insignias on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, exposed engine, and stacked biplane wings with cross insignias.
description_view_1: Left-front angled view highlighting the open cockpit, side insignia, and the rounded tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, both wings' full span, and the cross insignias on the upper wing.
description_view_3: Right-rear angled view showing the tail, side insignia, and the landing gear arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a small windshield.
